verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) is canonically an ER-luminal chaperone retained by a C-terminal KDEL motif with no constitutive TM anchor or GPI signal. Under ER stress (hypoxia, nutrient deprivation, oncogenic transformation, viral infection), it escapes ER retrieval via unconventional vesicular/endosomal trafficking and is displayed on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. Cell-surface HSPA5 (csGRP78) is well-documented by FACS, surface biotinylation, and cancer-patient autoantibodies; it is therapeutically targeted by cyclic peptides, antibodies, and ADCs on tumor cells. Display is strictly state-dependent (ER stress / cancer / infection), qualifying as cell_state_induced contextual.
